steps.title: Identify the contrast and evidence
steps.content:
The passage sets up a seeming threat: “the internet appeared to replace the zine, but this old form persists.” The evidence supporting this claim is stated immediately after—there are enough zines today to support annual festivals.

The passage is building toward an inference about what this persistence means.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the logical conclusion
steps.content:
If zines persist and thrive enough to fuel annual festivals despite the internet existing, the strongest inference is that zines still serve a real purpose for creators. zines are still a meaningful form of expression.—”zines are still a meaningful form of expression”—directly explains why zines would survive and grow rather than disappear.

creators can reach a larger audience by posting online. reverses the passage's logic by suggesting online posting is the solution; the passage shows zines remain valuable as printed objects. creators can continue to explore new art forms. is too generic and sidesteps the core point about zines themselves. zines are good sources of mainstream culture. contradicts the passage's claim that zine creators challenge (not reinforce) mainstream culture.

The festival evidence only makes sense if zines remain meaningful to creators and audiences. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
